title: Nottingham Triple Killer Assaulted Husband and Wife Weeks Before Attacks
summary: A public inquiry into the deaths of three people in Nottingham has heard that the triple killer, Valdo Calocane, assaulted two colleagues weeks before the attacks.

title: HMS Dragon Deploys to Cyprus Amid Tensions in the Mediterranean
summary: The Royal Navy's HMS Dragon has set sail from Portsmouth and is en route to Cyprus, where it will be deployed to the Mediterranean region in response to recent tensions.

title: Italy Acquires Rare Caravaggio Portrait for €30 Million
summary: The Italian state has purchased a rare Caravaggio painting for €30 million, part of a plan to prevent major artworks from being bought by private collectors.

title: Neil Simpson and Guide Rob Poth Win Alpine Combined Silver at Winter Paralympics
summary: Neil Simpson and guide Rob Poth have won Great Britain's first medal at the Winter Paralympics with a silver in the alpine combined. They have two further medal chances later in the Games.

title: Government Appoints Donna Ockenden as Leeds Hospitals Trust Maternity Inquiry Chair
summary: The UK government has appointed Donna Ockenden as the chair of an independent inquiry into maternity failures at Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust, following a campaign by bereaved and harmed familie

title: Signal Warns of Scams as Hackers Target Officials and Users
summary: Signal has warned its users to be cautious of scams after hackers targeted high-profile users, including officials and journalists, using sophisticated phishing campaigns.

title: Inmate Charged with Murder of Notorious Killer Ian Huntley
summary: A prison inmate has been charged with the murder of Ian Huntley, the former school caretaker who murdered 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002.

title: Family of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
summary: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI, alleging the company failed to alert authorities to the suspect's plans.

title: Violence Against Women: A Growing Epidemic in the UK
summary: A recent investigation has found that at least 90 women or girls were killed by men in the UK in 2025, with 24 resulting in convictions for murder and six for manslaughter.

title: Anthropic Sues US Government Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
summary: Anthropic sues US government over 'supply chain risk' label, citing 'unprecedented and unlawful' action.

title: London Offers Alternative to US for Embattled AI Firm Anthropic
summary: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan invites AI firm Anthropic to expand its presence in the city, amidst its deteriorating relationship with the US government.

title: Sleep-Enhancing Snacks Gain Popularity Amid Rising Demand for Better Rest
summary: A mushroom-infused chocolate brand has gained popularity with its sleep-enhancing product, Nightcap, which boasts a list of ingredients that claim to improve sleep quality. The demand for sleep-enhanc

title: TfL Hack in 2024 Exposed Data of 10 Million People, BBC Investigation Reveals
summary: A 2024 hack on Transport for London exposed the personal data of around 10 million people, the BBC has discovered, making it one of the biggest hacks in British history.

title: Musk Defends Social Media Posts in California Trial
summary: Elon Musk is defending himself against claims from investors who accuse him of trying to manipulate markets with misleading social media posts. Musk says his posts were 'extremely literal' and not int
